title: bugsweep session-summary
description: Deterministic aggregate of N bugsweep run-summary.json objects (bench.scorer.session_summary.merge_summaries), produced by scripts/aggregate-summaries.sh. Gives a headless scheduler one session-level view (totals, cross-run clusters, deduped follow-up, worst-status roll-up) instead of re-deriving it from N separate files. schema_version bumps only on a breaking change (see run-summary.schema.json's versioning rule, which this mirrors).

degraded.description: True only when the no-python aggregation path (scripts/aggregate-summaries.sh) emitted a minimal valid aggregate instead of the full merge.

root_cause_clusters.description: Cross-run clusters re-derived from every run's findings[], grouped by category with the size>=2 threshold applied at the session level (so a category that was a singleton per-run but reaches 2 across runs surfaces here). Size is the count of contributing findings, files is their union, representative is the lexicographically smallest bug_id. Ordered size descending, then cluster name ascending.

follow_up.description: Concatenation of every run's follow_up[], deduped by (kind, ref) keeping the first occurrence.

runs.description: Per-run status, in the order the run-summary.json files were passed in.

run_count.description: Number of runs merged. run_count == 0 (with worst_status 'no_runs') means a scheduler matched no run-summary.json files — distinct from a genuine all-complete session.

worst_status.description: Roll-up of runs[]: 'no_runs' if there were no runs at all (a non-success sentinel a scheduler must not read as clean); 'complete' if every run completed; 'stalled' if every run stalled; 'partial' otherwise (mixed outcomes).
